reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): Phase 03's code reading is factually correct. The verify_kzg_proofs function returns Result<bool>, but line 2195-2199 uses .map_err()? which only handles Err cases and discards the Ok(bool) result. When verify_kzg_proofs returns Ok(false) (cryptographically invalid proofs but no processing error), the boolean false is extracted by ? and immediately discarded without any check. Compare with blob_sidecar validation at line 1962 which correctly uses ensure!() to check the boolean. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by sending a data_column_sidecar with invalid KZG proofs that return Ok(false), causing the invalid sidecar to be accepted and propagated. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e property PROP-6a4369e9-pre-003 from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json line 173 requires 'forall sidecar: accepted(sidecar) => verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs(sidecar) == true'. The code violates this by accepting sidecars when verification returns Ok(false). Severity: Per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json Critical criteria, this allows propagation of invalid erasure-coded data across the entire P2P network, potentially affecting data availability sampling integrity for all nodes. Fusaka-specific PeerDAS validation bug, in-scope.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Gossip validation must reject sidecar with invalid KZG proofs. Assertion: forall sidecar: accepted(sidecar) => verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs(sidecar) == true (01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:173-188)
